 Qual (B) / Qual (C): If the initial 12-month validity period is exceeded, the PIC may continue to operate as Qual
(B2) / Qual (C2) - as applicable - for further 12 months with increased weather minima – see 6. WEATHER/AIRPORT
LIMITATIONS - arrivals

 Qual (B2) / Qual (C2):
• If within the further 12 months the PIC flies as PF or PM 3x IFR approaches then they are considered fully
qualified Qual (B) / Qual (C) again and the standard 12-month will apply.
• If within the further 12 months the PIC fails to fly as PF or PM at least 3 IFR approaches the Qual (B) / (C)
qualification is lost, and a full initial qualification is required.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND

If within the further 12 months the PIC fails to fly as PF or PM at least 3 IFR approaches the Qual (B) / (C)
qualification is lost, and a full initial qualification is required.

sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
REW QUALIFICATIONS:

There are 5 types of pilot qualifications for Lugano:
Qualification (A)
- which permits DAY only operation with a high weather minima
Qualification (B)
- which permits landing RWY19 DAY/NIGHT with lower weather minima
Qualification (B2) - which permits landing RWY19 DAY/NIGHT with increased weather minima (+500ft ceiling
and +1000m visibility) when compared to Qual (B)
- this qualification level is internally defined by NetJets, capturing when extending Qual (B)
beyond 12 months but less than 24 months validity.
Qualification (C)
- which permits the IGS 01 approach
Qualification (C2) - which permits the IGS 01 approach with increased weather minima (+500ft ceiling and
+1000m visibility) when compared to Qual (C)
- this qualification level is internally defined by NetJets, capturing when extending Qual (C)
beyond 12 months but less than 24 months validity.
Qualification (D)
- which permits take-off RWY19 at NIGHT with lower weather minima

For all operations at LSZA the PF shall hold a current AHQ.
NTA requires that all flight crew members on fleets which operate to LSZA must hold a Qual (A), which consists
of an on-line briefing plus a test for a PIC/SIC, and additionally NetJets simulator training for Qual (A) PICs.
For arrivals and/or departures requiring qualification (B), (B2), (C), (C2) or (D), the PF shall hold a PIC qualification
(B), (B2), (C) or (D) as applicable to the operation but may fly from either seat.
Holding Qual (A) also gives the qualification to operate as SIC / PM for Qual (B), (B2), (C), (C2) and (D) operations.
CREW QUALIFICATION LEVEL

-
QUALIFICATION B/B2 EQUIPMENT REQUIREMENTS:

AIRCRAFT EQUIPMENT:
For operation to Qual (B) or Qual (B2) minimums, the following equipment shall be operational. This is in
addition to any possible MEL restrictions.
• EGPWS must be fully operational;
• Radio Altimeter system must be fully operational;
• 1 VOR system;
• 1 Mode A/C SSR Transponder;
• TCAS components must all be fully functional;
• 1 FMS with up-to-date Nav Database;
• All PFD & MFD screens must be operational (TAWS Relative Terrain enabled and displayed on the MFD);
• 2 GPS Sensors or 2 DME systems.

AERODROME EQUIPMENT:
For night landings the RWY lead-in lighting system (RLLS) must be operative.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
10-10-2P5

-

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
he aerodrome of Lugano is located at the South edge of the Italian Alps, 2.2 NM West of Lugano city and North of
the Ceresio lake.
The mountains North of the CTR are up to 7300 feet high, South of the CTR they are up to 4100 feet high.

Overview of the Airport

lines: he aerodrome of Lugano is located at the South edge of the Italian Alps, 2.2 NM West of Lugano city and North of
The mountains North of the CTR are up to 7300 feet high, South of the CTR they are up to 4100 feet high.

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
pecial procedures and authorisations are required to operate at Lugano. This briefing combines Lugano Airport and
NetJets requirements to detail which procedures NTA crew may use and which minima to fly to.

NetJets has 3 tailored Jeppesen Charts for Lugano which can all be found under the ‘CO’ Tab on the iPad EFB as follows:
• 10-7 - NETJETS Special Engine Fail RWY 01 VMC.
• 10-7A - NETJETS Special Engine Fail RWY 19 IMC/VMC.
• 10-7B - NETJETS Missed Apch Circling CHARLIE RWY 19.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
10-10-2P7

-

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1. GENERAL

LANDING RUNWAY 01
-
Minima and procedures are the same for Qual (A) and Qual (B) crew regardless of aircraft category, only a
visual approach as per 9.6 IFR Visual Approach RWY 01 is permitted.
-
The IGS RWY 01 as per Jeppesen 11-1 is for Qual (C) or Qual (C2) pilots in the CE-680A fleet ONLY.

LANDING RUNWAY 19
-
Qual (A)
o CAT B aircraft should normally expect to perform the LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 as per 9.2.
CIRCLING APPROACH TO RWY 19 – QUAL (A) [CAT B AIRCRAFT].
o CAT C aircraft should refer to 9.4 CATEGORY C AIRCRAFT - APPROACH RWY 19.
-
Qual (B) & Qual (B2)
o CAT B aircraft may land with a lower weather minima and also at night, see 9.3. CIRCLING APPROACH
TO RWY 19 – QUAL (B) & QUAL (B2) [CAT B AIRCRAFT].

NOISE ABATEMENT
Use of thrust reversers above idle should be limited to safety requirements (e.g. landing when the runway is reported
as wet) and exceptional operational conditions.

LATE DESCENT CLEARANCE
For all approaches, expect possible late handover to Lugano. Consider preparing the PINIK hold on arrival in case the
approach or descent clearance is delayed.

CAUTION
LEVEL VIOLATION HOTSPOT
JEPPESEN CHART 10-2B is difficult to read.  Note the ODINA 8L, LEGLO 5P & EKLIB 5P ‘hide’ the cleared descent level
and may cause LEVEL BUSTS.

LEGLO 5P & EKLIB 5P:          Minimum level between D29.0 BRL and PINIK is the Transition Level as given by LSZA
ATIS (or by ATC), only after PINIK the minimum level goes down to 6000ft.
ODINA 8L:                              Minimum level between LUGAN and D20.0 MMP is the Transition Level as given by LSZA
ATIS (or by ATC), only after D20.0 MMP the minimum level goes down to 6000ft.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
10-10-2P8

-
9.2. CIRCLING APPROACH TO RWY 19 – QUAL (A) [CAT B AIRCRAFT]
DAY ONLY
Weather Minima: Ceiling 3100ft / Visibility 5km

Execute the LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 as per Jeppesen 11-2, followed by circling approach FOXTROT as per
Jeppesen 19-11.

Circling CHARLIE is PROHIBITED for Qual (A) crew.

Circling FOXTROT
The Circling FOXTROT procedure is the preferential manoeuvre for noise abatement purposes when landing on RWY
19 (AIP AD 2.21 3.)

The following FMS waypoints can aid flying the Circling FOXTROT, although it should be remembered that this is a
visual procedure that shall be performed in accordance with the relevant Jeppesen chart – some of these waypoints
may already be present in the FMS, carefully check as it may require manual insertion.

FMS WPT
COORDINATES
DESCRIPTION
10ILU
N45 59.11 E008 54.03
Break-Off D1.7
32ARP
N46 02.61 E008 57.66 3.2 nm from ARP
D017B
N46 02.03 E008 55.51 1.65 nm Final 19
D017A
N46 01.34 E008 55.17
0.9 nm Final 19

See the ‘Circling FOXTROT RWY 19 - FMS Points’ picture for how these waypoints relate to the terrain.

Missed Approach / Balked landing

Up to MAPt:
Fly Published Missed Approach as per Jeppesen procedure.

After MAPt:
Maintain VMC and visually regain circling altitude and circling prescribed track to point LUGAN (in the
overhead). On passing LUGAN climb and follow SID track outbound to PINIK at 6000 ft.

EGPWS alerts
EGPWS alerts may be experienced whilst performing the circle-to-land procedure. They are most likely to occur during
the descending base leg. Crew response to EGPWS Alerts and Warnings shall always be in accordance with OM-A
8.3.5.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
10-10-2P9

-

Circling FOXTROT RWY 19 - FMS Points
4 DME

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P10

-
9.3. CIRCLING APPROACH TO RWY 19 – QUAL (B) & QUAL (B2) [CAT B AIRCRAFT]
Qual (B) or Qual (B2) crew may use the same procedures and minima as for Qual (A) crew, unless the weather
conditions require the use of lower minima permitted by Qual (B) or Qual (B2) or a night landing is required, in which
case the following weather minima shall apply:

QUAL (B)
QUAL (B2)
DAY
Ceiling 1700 ft
Visibility 3 km
Ceiling 2200 ft
Visibility 4 km
NIGHT

Ceiling 2200 ft
Visibility 6 km

CAUTION:
- Refer to aircraft and airport requirements on QUALIFICATION B/B2 EQUIPMENT REQUIREMENTS
- For night landings the RWY lead-in lighting system (RLLS) must be operative.

Execute the LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 as per Jeppesen 11-2, followed by circling approach CHARLIE to Runway

as wet) and exceptional operational conditions.
For all approaches, expect possible late handover to Lugano. Consider preparing the PINIK hold on arrival in case the
approach or descent clearance is delayed.
JEPPESEN CHART 10-2B is difficult to read.  Note the ODINA 8L, LEGLO 5P & EKLIB 5P ‘hide’ the cleared descent level
LEGLO 5P & EKLIB 5P:          Minimum level between D29.0 BRL and PINIK is the Transition Level as given by LSZA
ATIS (or by ATC), only after PINIK the minimum level goes down to 6000ft.
ODINA 8L:                              Minimum level between LUGAN and D20.0 MMP is the Transition Level as given by LSZA
ATIS (or by ATC), only after D20.0 MMP the minimum level goes down to 6000ft.
9.2. CIRCLING APPROACH TO RWY 19 – QUAL (A) [CAT B AIRCRAFT]
Execute the LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 as per Jeppesen 11-2, followed by circling approach FOXTROT as per
Circling CHARLIE is PROHIBITED for Qual (A) crew.
The Circling FOXTROT procedure is the preferential manoeuvre for noise abatement purposes when landing on RWY
The following FMS waypoints can aid flying the Circling FOXTROT, although it should be remembered that this is a
visual procedure that shall be performed in accordance with the relevant Jeppesen chart – some of these waypoints
may already be present in the FMS, carefully check as it may require manual insertion.
See the ‘Circling FOXTROT RWY 19 - FMS Points’ picture for how these waypoints relate to the terrain.
Fly Published Missed Approach as per Jeppesen procedure.
Maintain VMC and visually regain circling altitude and circling prescribed track to point LUGAN (in the
overhead). On passing LUGAN climb and follow SID track outbound to PINIK at 6000 ft.
EGPWS alerts may be experienced whilst performing the circle-to-land procedure. They are most likely to occur during
the descending base leg. Crew response to EGPWS Alerts and Warnings shall always be in accordance with OM-A
9.3. CIRCLING APPROACH TO RWY 19 – QUAL (B) & QUAL (B2) [CAT B AIRCRAFT]
Qual (B) or Qual (B2) crew may use the same procedures and minima as for Qual (A) crew, unless the weather
conditions require the use of lower minima permitted by Qual (B) or Qual (B2) or a night landing is required, in which
case the following weather minima shall apply:
Refer to aircraft and airport requirements on QUALIFICATION B/B2 EQUIPMENT REQUIREMENTS
For night landings the RWY lead-in lighting system (RLLS) must be operative.
Execute the LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 as per Jeppesen 11-2, followed by circling approach CHARLIE to Runway

sections.title: Circling Charlie
sections.body:
he following FMS waypoints can aid flying the Circling CHARLIE, although it should be remembered that this is a
visual procedure that shall be performed in accordance with the relevant Jeppesen chart – some of these waypoints
may already be present in the FMS, carefully check as it may require manual insertion.

FMS WPT
COORDINATES
DESCRIPTION
10ILU
N45 59.11 E008 54.03
Break-Off D1.7
20ILU
N46 01.16 E008 56.95
2.0 nm ARP
D017B
N46 02.03 E008 55.51 1.65 nm Final 19
D017A
N46 01.34 E008 55.17
0.9 nm Final 19

See the ‘Circling CHARLIE RWY 19 - FMS Points’ picture for how these waypoints relate to the terrain.

Missed Approach / Balked landing

Up to MAPt:
Fly Published Missed Approach as per Jeppesen Procedure.

After MAPt:
Fly NetJets Missed Approach procedure, as per Jeppesen (NetJets) 10-7B. If it is certain that you are on the
published circling Charlie track, then it is permissible to perform the following level turn procedure:
-
Maintain published track, configuration, speed (max 135 kts), and altitude to 20ILU point.
-
Perform level left hand turn to 197° track inbound to RWY 19.
-
When wings level clean aircraft configuration, climb, accelerate, and follow SID track outbound to PINIK at
6000 ft. This level turn procedure requires much less energy than the normal missed approach procedure
and makes containing the speed easier.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P11

-
EGPWS alerts
EGPWS alerts may be experienced whilst performing the circle to land procedure. They are most likely to occur during
the descending base leg. Crew response to EGPWS Alerts and Warnings shall always be in accordance with OM-A
8.3.5.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P12

-

Circling CHARLIE RWY 19 - FMS Points
3 DME

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P13

-
9.4. CATEGORY C AIRCRAFT – APPROACH RWY 19
(as per 9.2 Circling Approach to RWY 19 – Qual (A) [CAT B Aircraft]) provided ALL speeds shown on Jeppesen 11-2 are
adhered to. If all of these speeds cannot be maintained or if there is any doubt, then the IFR Visual approach
procedure and visual approach minima must be used.

9.5. IFR VISUAL APPROACH RWY 19
Normally, an IFR visual approach RWY 19 will only be flown by a CAT C aircraft that is unable to comply with the
speeds shown on Jeppesen 11-2 (see 9.4 Category C Aircraft - Approach RWY 19):
• DAY ONLY.
• Weather Minima:  Ceiling 5100 ft
Visibility 8 km.
Consider visually following the vertical and lateral profile of LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 followed by circling
RWY 19.

CAUTION:
• Descent below 6000 ft whilst south of PINIK is PROHIBITED. This is due to interaction with Milan Malpensa
departures which level off at 5000 ft south of PINIK.
• The published Circle-to-land prescribed flight tracks do not guarantee CAT C obstacle clearance, so they should
be used as guidance only. Consider using Circle FOXTROT as guidance with a slightly wider base leg to allow for
the increased CAT C turn radius.
• The runway is approx. 8 nm / 14.4 km from PINIK, so with 8 km visibility, the runway might not be visible.

MISSED APPROACH / BALKED LANDING:

Up to LOC RWY 01 / CIRCLING RWY 19 MAPt:
Visually regain Missed Approach procedure as per Jeppesen 11-2.

After LOC RWY 01 / CIRCLING RWY 19 MAPt:
Maintain VMC and visually regain circling altitude and circling track to point LUGAN (in the overhead). On
passing LUGAN climb and follow SID track outbound to PINIK at 6000ft.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P15

-
9.6. IFR VISUAL APPROACH RWY 01
• DAY ONLY
• Weather Minima:
Ceiling 5100 ft
Visibility 8 km.
• CAT B & CAT C Aircraft.

Check with ATC that the 4.17° PAPI are switched on.

This approach is a visual approach with no prescribed tracks or vertical profile, the suggested ground tracks (red
dashed line) and vertical profile depicted in pictures ‘Visual Approach RWY 01’ and ‘View from 8.0 ILU at 4105 ft’ may
only be used to aid briefing / situational awareness.
Once visual and cleared for a visual approach, initiate a descent aiming to intercept the 4.17° PAPI by 3.3 ILU (2.5 nm
from the threshold) latest, the Altitudes and (Heights) given on the picture ‘Visual Approach RWY 01’ show the 4.17°
vertical path which will be intercepted from above.

CAUTION:
• Descent below 6000 ft whist south of PINIK is PROHIBITED. This is due to interaction with Milan Malpensa
departures which level off at 5000 ft south of PINIK.
• If the approach starts directly from PINIK at 6000 ft, then a steep descent (circa 6.99°) is required to intercept the
4.17° PAPI by 3.3 ILU (a table in picture ‘Visual Approach RWY 01’ gives Groundspeed vs Rate of Descent required
for a 6.99° descent).
• The RWY is approx. 8 nm / 14.4 km from PINIK, so with 8 km visibility, the runway might not be visible.

To manage the descent angle required to intercept the 4.17° PAPI consider adding some track miles to the approach,
initially using a higher rate of descent or performing an orbit just north of PINIK.

MISSED APPROACH / BALKED LANDING:

Up to D1.9 ILU:
Visually join the LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 missed approach procedure as per Jepp. 11-2.

After D1.9 ILU:
If performance permits it, visually regain Missed Approach procedure as per Jeppesen 11-2. If performance is
not assured, then visually follow OEI procedure as per PDCS chart ‘01DP4’.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P16

-

VISUAL APPROACH RWY 01

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P17

-

VIEW FROM 8.0 ILU AT 4105FT

FINAL RWY 01

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P18

-
9.7. IGS RWY 01
CE-680A ONLY
NOTE: CE-680A is certified for steep approaches up to 6.65° for tracking and 6° for landing.

QUALIFICATION:

Crew Qualification Level
Requirement
´My currency´ annotation
PIC
SIC (or PIC acting as SIC)
Qual (C)
LSZA - C - PIC
LSZA - SIC
Qual (C2)
LSZA – C2 - PIC
LSZA - SIC

WEATHER MINIMA:

QUAL (C)
QUAL (C2)
As per Jeppesen
Chart 11-1
As per Jeppesen
Chart 11-1
Ceiling + 500ft
Visibility + 1000m

PROCEDURE:
The use of the instrument APCH procedure IGS 01 is subject to the following regulations:
a. The approach will be performed at an angle of 6.65° (Phase 1). The landing phase (Phase 2) is performed at
a maximum angle of 6°, with the assistance of the PAPI set to this value.
b. The aircraft must be established (with the correspondent VREF) on the approach, with an approach angle of
6° at latest by 500 ft AAL; in any other case, the approach procedure must be terminated and a go-around
initiated.
c. The approach may only be performed if the tailwind component at the LDG threshold does not exceed half
of the value of the maximum tailwind component permitted, in accordance with the AFM for steep APCH
procedures.
d. The maximum permitted error above the glide path must correspond to no more than half-scale on the
glide-slope indicator (generally 1 dot). In a case where this limit is exceeded, a go-around procedure must
be immediately initiated.

PAPI RWY 01
There are two PAPIs for RWY 01. For all approaches, only one PAPI shall be illuminated and operative. The 6° PAPI on
RWY 01 will only be in use for IFR traffic performing an IGS approach.
For all other approaches, the 4.17° PAPI on RWY 01 will be in use. If on an IGS approach for a circling to land on RWY
19, the aircraft may descend onto the 6.0° PAPI earlier than the MDA or Visual Descent Point (VDP) but never before
3 DME ILU.

MISSED APPROACH / BALKED LANDING
One Engine Out – Follow the procedure on CO Jepp chart 10-7.
All Engines – As per IGS 01 chart 11-1.

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P19

-

lines: he following FMS waypoints can aid flying the Circling CHARLIE, although it should be remembered that this is a
visual procedure that shall be performed in accordance with the relevant Jeppesen chart – some of these waypoints
may already be present in the FMS, carefully check as it may require manual insertion.
See the ‘Circling CHARLIE RWY 19 - FMS Points’ picture for how these waypoints relate to the terrain.
Fly Published Missed Approach as per Jeppesen Procedure.
Fly NetJets Missed Approach procedure, as per Jeppesen (NetJets) 10-7B. If it is certain that you are on the
published circling Charlie track, then it is permissible to perform the following level turn procedure:
Maintain published track, configuration, speed (max 135 kts), and altitude to 20ILU point.
Perform level left hand turn to 197° track inbound to RWY 19.
When wings level clean aircraft configuration, climb, accelerate, and follow SID track outbound to PINIK at
6000 ft. This level turn procedure requires much less energy than the normal missed approach procedure
and makes containing the speed easier.
EGPWS alerts may be experienced whilst performing the circle to land procedure. They are most likely to occur during
the descending base leg. Crew response to EGPWS Alerts and Warnings shall always be in accordance with OM-A
(as per 9.2 Circling Approach to RWY 19 – Qual (A) [CAT B Aircraft]) provided ALL speeds shown on Jeppesen 11-2 are
adhered to. If all of these speeds cannot be maintained or if there is any doubt, then the IFR Visual approach
procedure and visual approach minima must be used.
LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 as
Visual approach as per 9.5 IFR Visual
Normally, an IFR visual approach RWY 19 will only be flown by a CAT C aircraft that is unable to comply with the
speeds shown on Jeppesen 11-2 (see 9.4 Category C Aircraft - Approach RWY 19):
Consider visually following the vertical and lateral profile of LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 followed by circling
Descent below 6000 ft whilst south of PINIK is PROHIBITED. This is due to interaction with Milan Malpensa
departures which level off at 5000 ft south of PINIK.
The published Circle-to-land prescribed flight tracks do not guarantee CAT C obstacle clearance, so they should
be used as guidance only. Consider using Circle FOXTROT as guidance with a slightly wider base leg to allow for
the increased CAT C turn radius.
The runway is approx. 8 nm / 14.4 km from PINIK, so with 8 km visibility, the runway might not be visible.
Up to LOC RWY 01 / CIRCLING RWY 19 MAPt:
Visually regain Missed Approach procedure as per Jeppesen 11-2.
After LOC RWY 01 / CIRCLING RWY 19 MAPt:
Maintain VMC and visually regain circling altitude and circling track to point LUGAN (in the overhead). On
passing LUGAN climb and follow SID track outbound to PINIK at 6000ft.
Check with ATC that the 4.17° PAPI are switched on.
This approach is a visual approach with no prescribed tracks or vertical profile, the suggested ground tracks (red
dashed line) and vertical profile depicted in pictures ‘Visual Approach RWY 01’ and ‘View from 8.0 ILU at 4105 ft’ may
only be used to aid briefing / situational awareness.
Once visual and cleared for a visual approach, initiate a descent aiming to intercept the 4.17° PAPI by 3.3 ILU (2.5 nm
from the threshold) latest, the Altitudes and (Heights) given on the picture ‘Visual Approach RWY 01’ show the 4.17°
vertical path which will be intercepted from above.
Descent below 6000 ft whist south of PINIK is PROHIBITED. This is due to interaction with Milan Malpensa
departures which level off at 5000 ft south of PINIK.
If the approach starts directly from PINIK at 6000 ft, then a steep descent (circa 6.99°) is required to intercept the
4.17° PAPI by 3.3 ILU (a table in picture ‘Visual Approach RWY 01’ gives Groundspeed vs Rate of Descent required
The RWY is approx. 8 nm / 14.4 km from PINIK, so with 8 km visibility, the runway might not be visible.
To manage the descent angle required to intercept the 4.17° PAPI consider adding some track miles to the approach,
initially using a higher rate of descent or performing an orbit just north of PINIK.
Visually join the LOC RWY 01 for CIRCLING RWY 19 missed approach procedure as per Jepp. 11-2.
If performance permits it, visually regain Missed Approach procedure as per Jeppesen 11-2. If performance is
not assured, then visually follow OEI procedure as per PDCS chart ‘01DP4’.
NOTE: CE-680A is certified for steep approaches up to 6.65° for tracking and 6° for landing.
The use of the instrument APCH procedure IGS 01 is subject to the following regulations:
a. The approach will be performed at an angle of 6.65° (Phase 1). The landing phase (Phase 2) is performed at
a maximum angle of 6°, with the assistance of the PAPI set to this value.
b. The aircraft must be established (with the correspondent VREF) on the approach, with an approach angle of
6° at latest by 500 ft AAL; in any other case, the approach procedure must be terminated and a go-around
c. The approach may only be performed if the tailwind component at the LDG threshold does not exceed half
of the value of the maximum tailwind component permitted, in accordance with the AFM for steep APCH
d. The maximum permitted error above the glide path must correspond to no more than half-scale on the
glide-slope indicator (generally 1 dot). In a case where this limit is exceeded, a go-around procedure must
There are two PAPIs for RWY 01. For all approaches, only one PAPI shall be illuminated and operative. The 6° PAPI on
RWY 01 will only be in use for IFR traffic performing an IGS approach.
For all other approaches, the 4.17° PAPI on RWY 01 will be in use. If on an IGS approach for a circling to land on RWY
19, the aircraft may descend onto the 6.0° PAPI earlier than the MDA or Visual Descent Point (VDP) but never before
One Engine Out – Follow the procedure on CO Jepp chart 10-7.
All Engines – As per IGS 01 chart 11-1.

sections.title: Departure procedures
sections.body:
0.1. GENERAL

DEPARTURE RWY 01:
-
the minima and procedures are the same for Qual (A) and Qual (D) crew.

DEPARTURE RWY 19:
-
Qual (D) crew may depart with a lower weather minima and also at night. However, as per PDCS chart,
departing under these conditions will result in a lower maximum takeoff mass than under Qual (A) departure
conditions.
-
Qual (D) crews may depart using Qual (A) procedures and minima (as per 10.3 Departure Runway 19 (Qual
(A))) if a higher take-off mass is required.

10.2. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 01

• DAY ONLY
• Weather Minima:
Ceiling 4100 ft
Visibility 5 km.

PERFORMANCE: As per PDCS chart ‘01DP4’.

NORMAL PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen. On the SID, note the requirement to maintain Visual ground Contact until
point LUGAN (Airfield overhead). LUGAN also has an SID Altitude restriction of at or above 5000 ft (4085 ft above
Airport).

CONTINGENCY RWY 01: See Jeppesen (NetJets) 10-7 & PDCS chart ‘01DP4’. Note that the contingency procedure
requires VMC to be maintained until established outbound on the ILU LOC, once established outbound on the LOC
a continuous climb (into IMC if required) can be made.

CAUTION:
CANE2U SID RWY 01: TRACK DEVIATION HOTSPOT IN HIGH TERRAIN

Initial Climb:
Comply with 'INITIAL CLIMB' description on JEPP 10-3B. Remain on the published track (right
turn) until 'LUGAN'.
DO NOT turn LEFT to 'CANNE' even if you have reached 5000´prior to 'LUGAN'.

Routing:
At 'LUGAN' and above 5000´ turn LEFT intercept SRN R347 to 'CANNE'. (FMS coding ZA505,
ZA506)

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P20

-

CANE2U SID RWY 01 - TRACK DEVIATION HOTSPOT IN HIGH TERRAIN

10.3. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 19 (QUAL (A))
• DAY ONLY
• Weather Minima:
Ceiling 2100 ft
Visibility 3000 m.

PERFORMANCE: As per PDCS chart ‘19DP’.

NORMAL PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen.

CONTINGENCY RWY 19: See Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7A ‘QUAL (A) VMC’ procedure and PDCS chart ‘19DP’.

10.4. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 19 (QUAL (D))
• DAY or NIGHT.
• Weather Minima:
Ceiling not required
Visibility 400 m.

PERFORMANCE: As per PDCS chart ‘19DP’.

NORMAL PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen.

CONTINGENCY RWY 19: See Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7A ‘QUAL (D) IMC’ procedure and PDCS ‘19DP’ chart.

Initial Climb
Routing

LUGANO, SWITZERLAND
LUGANO
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P21

-

sections.title: Miscellaneous
sections.body:
EATHER CHARACTERISTICS - please see http://www.lugano-qualification.ch
GLIDER ACTIVITY: The Mountains surrounding the airport are potential areas for gliding and para-gliding activity.
Special attention should be exercise near PINIK and CALDO during spring and summer.
Visit the site http://www.lugano-qualification.ch for any further information and photographs.

lines: GLIDER ACTIVITY: The Mountains surrounding the airport are potential areas for gliding and para-gliding activity.
Special attention should be exercise near PINIK and CALDO during spring and summer.
Visit the site http://www.lugano-qualification.ch for any further information and photographs.

sections.title: Changes since the last revision
sections.body:
Change to the Lugano qualifications.
- Introduction of new qualification levels Qual (B2), Qual (C2) and Qual (D).
- Integration of safety alerts related to STAR level bust and RWY01 SID track deviation.
Provide feedback to Lis-OpsTechnical@netjets.com

lines: Introduction of new qualification levels Qual (B2), Qual (C2) and Qual (D).
Integration of safety alerts related to STAR level bust and RWY01 SID track deviation.
